states.state_name: Turn on AutoFilter from the Data menu
states.when_to_use: Use this card when LibreOffice Calc is already focused on the worksheet table and you still need to begin the AutoFilter flow from the menu bar. It is the pre-menu state: the sheet is visible, the data range is active, and the Data menu label is the next interaction cue before any filter arrows appear in the header row.
states.when_not_to_use: Do not use this card if the Data menu is already open, if header filter dropdown arrows are already visible on the live table, or if you are inside the Standard Filter dialog or another non-worksheet surface.
visible_cues: LibreOffice Calc is on the worksheet with a contiguous table visible and selected.
The Data menu label in the top menu bar is highlighted as the next place to act.
The table is still shown in plain worksheet view without visible header filter dropdown controls.
non_transferable_parts: The workbook title, formula bar contents, and the active cell address belong to the source example only.
Example data such as city names, years, house-price fields, and volume fields must not be copied into a new task unless the prompt explicitly asks for them.
The exact selected cells, table extent, and any box positions in the screenshot are not reusable coordinates.
states.recommended_verification: Before acting, verify that you are still on the worksheet table itself and that enabling AutoFilter has not happened yet on the current range.

evidence_source.source_instruction: Calculate the covariance between the Average house price (yuan/square meter) and the Volume. Add an automatic filter to all the data and sort the "City" column in ascending order.
evidence_source.source_action: Click on the "Data" menu in the top menu bar.
evidence_source.procedure_name: Enable AutoFilter and prepare the table for sorting or quick filtering
evidence_source.state_name: Turn on AutoFilter from the Data menu

states.state_name: Verify the table headers now expose filter controls
states.when_to_use: Use this card when the data range is already selected and the Data menu is open, with AutoFilter available to click. This is the actionable menu-open state for turning on header filters before any later sort or row-filtering work.
states.when_not_to_use: Do not use this card if header filter arrows are already active on the worksheet, if the Data menu is closed, or if the current task has already moved on to choosing filter criteria or validating the filtered result.
visible_cues: The Data menu dropdown is open over the worksheet.
AutoFilter is visible in the menu as the relevant command.
The selected table or header region remains marked behind the menu, showing which range the command will affect.
non_transferable_parts: The menu position, table crop, and all box placements are example-specific and must not be reused as coordinates.
The workbook name, formula text, and the real-estate worksheet contents are source-example details only.
The exact columns and visible rows behind the menu are not part of the transferable procedure.
states.recommended_verification: Verify that the open menu is anchored to Data and that AutoFilter is available for the currently selected range before clicking it.

states.state_name: Verify that only the intended rows remain visible after filtering
states.when_to_use: Use this card after the filter logic has already been applied and you need to verify the remaining visible rows on the worksheet. It is the post-filter result state, useful before later calculations, charts, or task completion.
states.when_not_to_use: Do not use this card as instructions for choosing criteria, and do not treat a merely shorter table as success. Skip it while a filter dialog is still open or before the requested keep-or-exclude logic has been applied.
visible_cues: LibreOffice Calc has returned to worksheet view rather than a menu or dialog.
Only a reduced subset of rows remains visible under the header row.
The visible table region is marked as the result area to inspect.
non_transferable_parts: The clothing terms, quarter labels, material names, and numeric values shown in the table are example-specific.
The SUBTOTAL formula text and the emphasized sales column belong to the source example only.
The exact row numbers and the particular rows that remain visible in the screenshot must not be copied into a new task.
states.recommended_verification: Check that every still-visible row satisfies the current prompt's requested filter condition and that rows outside the requested subset are hidden before proceeding.

highlight_targets.name: Filtered visible rows
highlight_targets.annotation_query: the rows that remain visible after filtering in the LibreOffice Calc worksheet

evidence_source.source_instruction: Apply an auto-filter to all the data to filter out items in the Clothing categories that contain "Autumn" or "shirt", and then calculate the total Sales volume of these filtered data.
evidence_source.source_action: Press Enter to confirm and execute the SUBTOTAL formula that will calculate the sum of the filtered Sales volume values.
evidence_source.procedure_name: Configure Standard Filter logic and verify the visible rows
evidence_source.state_name: Verify that only the intended rows remain visible after filtering
